restaurant.name: Restaurant / Cafe
top_leak_patterns: No online reservation or ordering system — losing 18% of potential covers
No email capture for repeat customer promotions and specials
No visible hours or phone number above the fold
restaurant.language_style: direct and practical — restaurateurs are busy, respect their time, speak in covers and tables not clicks and conversions
restaurant.local_context: Berkshires restaurants serve both year-round locals and seasonal tourists from May-October, making online visibility critical during peak season

contractor.name: Contractor / Home Services
top_leak_patterns: No click-to-call phone number — contractors live and die by the phone call
No visible service area or emergency service availability
No customer reviews or testimonials visible on homepage
contractor.language_style: no-nonsense and results-focused — contractors are skeptical of marketing, lead with jobs won and revenue earned not impressions and click-through rates
contractor.local_context: Western MA contractors face strong seasonal demand — heating season in fall/winter and renovation season in spring/summer — missing online leads during peak periods is especially costly

auto_repair.name: Auto Repair / Detailing
top_leak_patterns: No online appointment booking — customers will call a competitor who has it
No visible pricing for common services like oil changes
No Google reviews integration or social proof
auto_repair.language_style: straight-talking and trust-focused — auto shop owners are practical people who understand that reputation is everything in a small town
auto_repair.local_context: In the Berkshires, word-of-mouth drives auto repair business but an online presence captures the tourist traffic and new residents who don't yet have a trusted mechanic

salon_barbershop.name: Salon / Barbershop
top_leak_patterns: No online booking system — clients book with whoever is easiest to reach
No stylist profiles or portfolio photos
No social media links showing current work
salon_barbershop.language_style: warm and relationship-focused — salon owners understand that clients choose a person not just a place, so speak to building personal connections online
salon_barbershop.local_context: Berkshires salons serve a loyal local base plus seasonal visitors — Instagram and online booking are the two highest-ROI investments for this market

dental_medical.name: Dental / Medical / Chiropractic
top_leak_patterns: No online appointment request form — patients default to whoever makes booking easiest
No insurance information visible — patients skip sites that make them guess
No patient reviews or testimonials — trust is everything in healthcare
dental_medical.language_style: professional and reassuring — healthcare providers want to be seen as trustworthy, use language about patient experience and practice growth not lead generation
dental_medical.local_context: Berkshires healthcare practices compete for a limited local population — every lost patient represents significant lifetime value, and new residents actively search online for providers

retail.name: Retail / Gift Shop
top_leak_patterns: No e-commerce or online ordering — missing sales from visitors who want to buy after their trip
No email list capture for new arrivals and seasonal promotions
No visible store hours or location with map
retail.language_style: visual and experience-driven — retail owners think in foot traffic and average ticket size, connect online presence to in-store visits
retail.local_context: Berkshires retail thrives on tourist season foot traffic from May-October, but an online presence extends revenue year-round and captures post-visit purchases from visitors who fell in love with a product

legal.name: Law Firm / Attorney
top_leak_patterns: No free consultation offer or intake form — potential clients call whoever makes it easiest
No practice area pages with clear descriptions of services
No attorney bios with credentials and experience
legal.language_style: authoritative but approachable — attorneys want to project competence, frame findings in terms of client acquisition cost and case value
legal.local_context: Western MA legal market is relationship-driven but increasingly competitive online — residents searching for attorneys start with Google, and the first firm with a clear intake process wins the client

real_estate.name: Real Estate / Property Management
top_leak_patterns: No IDX property search or featured listings on homepage
No lead capture for buyer/seller inquiries
No neighborhood or market data to establish local expertise
real_estate.language_style: market-savvy and relationship-oriented — agents think in transactions and commission, connect online presence to listing appointments and buyer consultations
real_estate.local_context: Berkshires real estate is driven by second-home buyers from NYC and Boston — these buyers research extensively online before contacting an agent, making web presence critical for capturing high-value leads

fitness.name: Fitness / Gym / Yoga
top_leak_patterns: No class schedule or online booking visible
No free trial or first-class-free offer
No member testimonials or transformation stories
fitness.language_style: energetic and community-focused — fitness studio owners think in memberships and retention, connect online presence to trial-to-member conversion
fitness.local_context: Berkshires fitness studios compete for a health-conscious community that values local, boutique experiences — a strong online presence differentiates from chain gyms

lodging.name: Inn / B&B / Lodging
top_leak_patterns: No direct booking engine — losing 15-20% commission to OTAs on every reservation
No visible room photos or virtual tour
No seasonal packages or special offers to drive direct bookings
lodging.language_style: hospitality-minded and revenue-focused — innkeepers understand RevPAR and occupancy rates, frame findings in terms of direct bookings vs OTA dependency
lodging.local_context: Berkshires lodging is highly seasonal with peak demand during fall foliage, Tanglewood season, and summer weekends — direct booking capability during these periods is the single highest-impact investment

ecommerce.name: E-Commerce / Online Store
top_leak_patterns: No clear call-to-action on product pages — shoppers abandon without adding to cart
No abandoned cart recovery or email capture at checkout
No trust badges, return policy, or shipping info visible during purchase flow
ecommerce.language_style: conversion-focused and data-driven — e-commerce operators think in AOV, conversion rate, and cart abandonment, speak their language
ecommerce.local_context: Online stores compete in a global marketplace where every friction point in the purchase flow costs real revenue — speed, trust signals, and checkout simplicity determine who wins

hotel_hospitality.name: Hotel / Hospitality
top_leak_patterns: No direct booking engine — paying 15-25% OTA commissions on every reservation
No visible room photos, amenities list, or virtual tour to drive direct bookings
No seasonal packages or special offers to compete with OTA pricing
hotel_hospitality.language_style: hospitality-minded and margin-focused — hoteliers think in RevPAR, occupancy rates, and OTA dependency, frame findings around direct booking revenue
hotel_hospitality.local_context: Hotels and hospitality businesses lose significant margin to OTA commissions — every direct booking recovered is pure profit improvement

financial_services.name: Financial Services / Advisory
top_leak_patterns: No consultation booking form — high-value prospects leave when they can't schedule immediately
No credentials, certifications, or regulatory compliance info visible — trust is everything in financial services
No educational content or lead magnets to capture prospects still in research phase
financial_services.language_style: professional and trust-centered — financial professionals think in AUM, client lifetime value, and referral rates, frame findings around qualified lead acquisition
financial_services.local_context: Financial services clients represent significant lifetime value — a single missed prospect can mean tens of thousands in lost recurring revenue over the relationship

marketing_agency.name: Marketing / Digital Agency
top_leak_patterns: No case studies or portfolio with measurable results — agencies must prove ROI to win clients
No clear service pages with pricing tiers or engagement models
No lead qualification form to filter prospects by budget and needs
marketing_agency.language_style: results-oriented and metric-driven — agency owners think in retainer value, client acquisition cost, and churn rate, they need to see you speak their language
marketing_agency.local_context: Marketing agencies face the irony of helping clients with marketing while often neglecting their own — a strong website is their most important sales asset

photography_videography.name: Photography / Videography
top_leak_patterns: No portfolio gallery with recent work — visual businesses must show, not tell
No booking form or availability calendar — clients book the first photographer who responds
No pricing guide or package information — prospects leave to find someone more transparent
photography_videography.language_style: visual and experience-focused — photographers think in bookings, session value, and seasonal demand, connect online presence to booking conversion
photography_videography.local_context: Photography and video businesses are inherently visual — their website IS their portfolio, and a weak online presence directly costs bookings

cleaning_services.name: Cleaning / Janitorial Services
top_leak_patterns: No instant quote or booking form — cleaning customers choose the easiest provider to hire
No visible service area map or zip code checker
No before/after photos or customer reviews — trust is critical for in-home services
cleaning_services.language_style: simple and trust-focused — cleaning service owners think in recurring clients and average job value, speak practically about growing their client base
cleaning_services.local_context: Cleaning services live and die by trust and convenience — customers let these people into their homes, so reviews, background check badges, and easy booking are non-negotiable

food_truck.name: Food Truck / Catering
top_leak_patterns: No schedule or location tracker — customers can't find you if they don't know where you'll be
No catering inquiry form or event booking capability
No menu with photos and pricing — people eat with their eyes first
food_truck.language_style: energetic and hustle-focused — food truck operators think in daily revenue, event bookings, and repeat customers, keep it practical and action-oriented
food_truck.local_context: Food trucks and catering businesses depend on discoverability and event bookings — a website that shows where you are, what you serve, and how to book is essential

childcare_education.name: Childcare / Education
top_leak_patterns: No enrollment inquiry form or waitlist signup — parents research extensively and need an easy next step
No visible licensing, certifications, or safety credentials — parents need reassurance
No photos of facility, staff bios, or curriculum overview — parents need to see where their children will be
childcare_education.language_style: warm and trust-building — childcare operators know parents are making one of their most important decisions, frame everything around safety, quality, and peace of mind
childcare_education.local_context: Childcare and education businesses have high lifetime value per family — a single enrolled child often represents years of monthly tuition, making every missed inquiry extremely costly

veterinary.name: Veterinary / Pet Care
top_leak_patterns: No online appointment booking — pet owners book with whoever is easiest to reach in a crisis
No emergency hours or after-hours contact info visible — this is life-or-death for pet owners
No staff bios, facility photos, or patient reviews — pet owners need to trust who handles their animals
veterinary.language_style: compassionate and trust-focused — vet clinic owners understand the emotional bond between pets and owners, frame findings around patient retention and new client acquisition
veterinary.local_context: Veterinary and pet care businesses benefit from high client loyalty — once a pet owner trusts a provider, they stay for years, making each new client acquisition extremely valuable

moving_storage.name: Moving / Storage
top_leak_patterns: No instant quote form or moving calculator — customers get quotes from the first 3 companies that respond
No visible licensing, insurance, and bonding information — trust is everything when strangers handle your belongings
No reviews or ratings integration — moving is one of the most review-dependent industries
moving_storage.language_style: straightforward and trust-building — moving company owners know customers are anxious about the process, frame findings around speed-to-quote and trust signals
moving_storage.local_context: Moving and storage businesses are highly commoditized — the company that responds fastest with a clear quote wins the job, making website speed-to-lead the single biggest differentiator

default.name: Local Business
top_leak_patterns: No clear call-to-action telling visitors what to do next
No visible contact information above the fold
No social proof or customer testimonials
default.language_style: clear and business-focused — speak in terms of customers gained and revenue earned, not marketing jargon
default.local_context: Western MA small businesses rely on a mix of loyal locals and seasonal visitors — a strong online presence serves both audiences year-round
